name: bbolt
description: bbolt is the maintained community fork of BoltDB, an embedded key/value database for Go. It stores data in a single memory-mapped file using a B+Tree structure, providing fully serializable ACID transactions with MVCC via copy-on-write semantics. Readers operate concurrently without blocking, while writes are serialized through a file lock. The pure-Go implementation is compact (under 5 KLOC) and has zero external dependencies. bbolt serves as the storage backend for etcd, the distributed key-value store that powers the Kubernetes control plane.
core_strengths: Pure Go implementation with zero external dependencies and under 5 KLOC
ACID transactions with serializable isolation via copy-on-write
Memory-mapped B+Tree enabling zero-copy reads from the OS buffer cache
Concurrent readers never block; writes serialized through a file-level lock
Nested bucket support for logical key-space partitioning and namespacing
best_use_cases: Embedded metadata and configuration storage in Go applications
Persistent cache that survives process restarts
Storage backend for distributed systems (e.g., etcd in Kubernetes)
Learning transactional key-value store internals from a clean, compact codebase
Single-file application state persistence without external database dependencies
remarks: bbolt is the maintained fork of BoltDB, which was archived by its original author in 2017. It is now maintained by the etcd-io organization and powers etcd, the distributed key-value store used by Kubernetes. Only one writer at a time is permitted (enforced via file lock), but multiple readers can operate concurrently. Keys within a bucket are stored in lexicographic byte order, enabling efficient prefix and range scans. The database file must be opened by a single process; sharing across processes requires opening read-only or using OS-level locking coordination.
